positions.text: One can have two moral obligations whose behavioral discharge coincides.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A single act may discharge multiple distinct moral obligations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Conflicting obligations may require violating one to fulfill another.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Moral obligation to honor agreements is distinct from moral obligation to feed Rusty.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Identity of obligation depends on counterfactual dependencies involving different individuals (e.g., Rusty vs. Poppy).
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Distinct obligations may converge in present circumstances but diverge in hypothetical ones.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation not to kill is identical with the moral obligation to obey laws that protect rights.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Moral obligation not to kill is stronger than the moral obligation to follow the law.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Behavior that satisfies the stronger obligation may satisfy the weaker, but not conversely.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A gunman’s command does not generate obligations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A gunman is not part of a system that provides social benefit.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Traffic laws are part of a system that does good.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Social benefit explains the normative weight of legal statements.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Laws are normatively pregnant; commands of criminals are not.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Laws presuppose a functioning government.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Government presupposes a moral structure.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Statements describing laws require background moral conditions to be true.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Normative content of legal statements cannot be morally antiseptic.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal normativity must be understood through moral normativity.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: This remains true even when some laws are immoral or empty.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Systems that protect rights generate legal obligations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal systems typically protect most of one’s rights.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal systems often prevent being killed, enslaved, or dehumanized.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Such protection morally matters for legal normativity.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The difference between law and gunman’s order is rooted in systemic benefit.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Normativity of law depends on moral background features.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Laws may fail to do much good, but still presuppose a moralized social structure.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Even wicked laws presuppose government and thus minimal moral architecture.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Normative pregnancy of legal statements depends on moral prerequisites.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Wicked laws do not eliminate the moral structure underlying legal normativity.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Some proposed theories of legal obligation appear plausible but are mistaken.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obedience to evil laws often does harm, not good.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Good done by obeying evil laws is accidental, not essential.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation cannot be grounded solely in benefits produced by obedience.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligations can exist even where obedience produces no benefit.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Moral obligation to comply with law varies with the goodness of the system.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Moral thresholds determine legal obligation status.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation is binary relative to threshold satisfaction.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Degree of moral obligation affects legal standing indirectly.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation requires the legal system to pass a moral threshold test.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obligations can be multiply realizable across behavioral contexts.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal norms apply categorically within a system.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation is contingent on lawful jurisdiction.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Moral obligation exists regardless of jurisdiction.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Two obligations may occupy identical behavioral space while being metaphysically separate.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Distinguishing obligations requires attention to counterfactuals.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Complete identity of obligations cannot be established by behavior alone.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obligations are individuated by their grounds, not by their outputs.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: When moral and legal obligations coincide, they do so non-identically.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A legal obligation is always morally grounded but not identical to the moral obligation it resembles.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Wicked systems can still create legal obligations if above threshold.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation depends on systemic good, not on specific law’s goodness.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Traffic laws illustrate lawful normativity without inherent moral content in the act-type.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal normativity differs fundamentally from accidental commands.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The moral structure of government supports legal obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligations track moral structure indirectly.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Normative content of legal statements presupposes sufficient moral order.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The appearance of moral triviality in minor legal duties is misleading.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation is morally grounded, binary, distinct in identity, and embedded within a protective system.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: “Ought implies can” is not universally valid.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: One can owe what one cannot deliver.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Debt persists even under impossibility of repayment.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obligation does not vanish because performance is impossible.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Moral obligation is not dependent on ability.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The feeling of remorse tracks a genuine norm.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A therapist can violate one obligation while fulfilling another.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: “Ought” is ambiguous in philosophical usage.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: There is an “ought” that tracks best available action.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: There is an “ought” that tracks fidelity to obligations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Remorse indicates recognition of a violated obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A moral conflict may generate two simultaneous “oughts.”
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Some obligations bind even when outweighed.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Moral obligations can persist under overridden conditions.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obligations differ by weight rather than by existence.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Some obligations outweigh but do not cancel others.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Rights also have a dimension of weight.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A trivial right may be outweighed without being erased.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A pharmacy owner retains a right even when outweighed.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A thief may still be morally wronged by being stopped.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Government acts are rarely wholly bad.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: One has obligations under a system only if protected by it.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Lack of protection eliminates legal obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Recognition by the legal system is tied to protection.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obligations arise from being recognized as a rights-holder.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A system that fails to protect someone gives them no obligations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A law may be unfair yet still do some good.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A law may be unfair in some respects and fair in others.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Government must protect all citizens, not only one.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A law beneficial to many may still burden one unfairly.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A law is on balance good if its protections outweigh its harms.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A government has a right to impose laws that are on balance good.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: That right may be weaker than the right to impose better laws.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Compliance with law is necessary for its functioning.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Non-compliance hinders legal functioning.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Even bad laws require compliance to function.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Compliance conduces to occurrence of a nontrivial good.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: One may have an obligation to comply even if outweighed.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The obligation to comply can be weaker than competing rights.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A citizen’s obligation to a law depends partly on dependence on the system.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Protection generates reciprocal moral ties.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation is partly grounded in dependence on a structure of protection.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Being personally protected deepens one’s obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obligations to benefactors are stronger than to strangers.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation is dual: systemic and personal.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A government’s role is protective by conceptual necessity.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A legal system exists to discharge protective functions.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A law exists in virtue of protecting or being meant to protect.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Some laws protect no moral good.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: That fact does not vindicate positivism.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Existence of pathological cases does not refute a theory.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Pathological counterexamples often confirm structural truths.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Evil governments still provide minimal protections.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Governmental evil presupposes dependence of citizens.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Evil uses the system’s protective role to cause harm.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal systems can be deeply harmful when corrupted.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Their capacity for harm stems from their centrality to protection.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The more essential the organ, the worse its malfunction.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal systems are essential organs of society.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Deep moral rootedness enables deep moral danger.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A dictator misuses legal structure to amplify harm.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal systems’ importance makes them easy tools of oppression.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Their misuse does not negate their moral foundation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A system’s ability to harm arises from its ability to protect.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal systems presuppose moral background structures.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Positivism ignores these presuppositions.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Laws require governments; governments require minimal morality.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A government must protect to count as one.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A legal system must provide some recognizable protections.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Even evil systems protect more than they destroy.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Dependence generates vulnerability.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Vulnerability explains moral stakes of legal structures.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Law’s existence is conceptually tied to moral goods.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Governmental recognition of citizens is a moral act.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Failure to recognize removes obligations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A system that fails to recognize someone gives them no duties.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Recognition depends on protection.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Protection is the currency of moral recognition.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A law can do good for someone it also harms.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Fairness and unfairness can coexist within a law.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The balance of moral considerations determines obligation strength.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Jones’ obligation persists though outweighed.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The right to flourish may outweigh legal compliance.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Weaker obligations still bind.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Government has differential strength of rights to impose systems.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: More enlightened systems generate stronger compliance duties.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Less enlightened systems generate weaker compliance duties.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Compliance supports the good a system provides.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Non-compliance undermines systemic functioning.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A system’s functioning produces goods people depend on.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Personal dependence amplifies one’s obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obligations to systems mirror obligations to benefactors.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Personal benefit increases moral weight.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Systems benefit both individually and collectively.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation is partly relational.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: One’s obligation is shaped by one’s role in systemic functioning.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obligations track both individual and collective goods.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obligations persist even when overridden by stronger rights.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: On-balance judgment does not erase underlying obligations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation’s structure reflects weighted, layered moral commitments.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation is identical with a certain kind of moral obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: That obligation is: to comply with a system of laws that does substantial good.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Moral obligations differ in kind even when behaviorally identical.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligations differ from moral obligations in identity, not in existence.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A single action can satisfy multiple distinct obligations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A single action can violate one obligation while fulfilling another.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation does not derive from individual laws taken in isolation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation derives from participation in a morally significant system.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The grounding obligation is system-directed, not act-directed.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The identity of obligations depends on what they are *about*.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obligations are individuated by their grounds rather than their outputs.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Behavioral equivalence does not entail identity of obligations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obligations must be compared at the level of moral structure, not surface conduct.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: When legal and moral obligations coincide, they remain conceptually distinct.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Reinforcement explains perceived differences in severity of legal duties.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The more independent moral reinforcement an obligation has, the more serious it appears.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligations have equal moral weight *as legal obligations*.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Differences in reinforcement create illusions of unequal moral importance.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation not to kill appears weighty because of independent moral obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation not to speed appears light because it lacks such reinforcement.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The moral obligation to save a life outweighs many lesser moral duties.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Outweighing does not imply cancellation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligations can be outweighed without being nullified.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Outweighing preserves the existence of an obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Rights also persist when outweighed.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A right can be infringed without being erased.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The structure of reasons mirrors the structure of rights and obligations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Weight is the essential comparative property of reasons.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal systems presuppose a normative structure that includes rights.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal rights track underlying moral rights.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal rights may diverge from full moral rights without losing authority.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal authority depends on systemic moral purpose.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Systems lacking protective purpose cannot ground obligations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: To be protected is to be recognized.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Recognition is a moral act.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obligation flows from recognition.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Where recognition is absent, obligation cannot arise.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A legal system must treat an individual as within its moral constituency.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Membership in a legal system is morally loaded.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A system’s protective function creates relational obligations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Reciprocity is essential to legal obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Protection and compliance are reciprocal normative acts.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A system protects an individual; the individual owes system-compliance.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Compliance is the proper moral response to being protected.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation is grounded in reciprocity, not coercion.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Even evil systems protect enough to establish minimal reciprocity.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Pathological cases presuppose normal cases.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Therefore pathological cases cannot ground general theory.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The capacity to do great harm presupposes the capacity to do great good.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal systems possess deep moral significance because they structure social survival.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal normativity is unintelligible without moral normativity.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Descriptive accounts of law smuggle in moral assumptions.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal statements are normatively pregnant.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Law requires an implicit “ought.”
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A merely descriptive command cannot constitute a law.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A bare coercive threat cannot generate obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obligations arise only from morally structured authorities.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Authority presupposes legitimacy.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legitimacy is always moral, never merely factual.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A dictator’s law retains minimal legitimacy through retained protections.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Total illegitimacy is possible only when protections collapse entirely.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: In such cases, legal obligation disappears.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A legal system’s functioning depends on compliance.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Compliance is a collective good.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Collective goods impose obligations on participants.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obligations regarding collective goods track dependence relations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Individuals depend on collective goods for core rights.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Dependence amplifies obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Failure to comply diminishes collective goods.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Non-compliance threatens others’ rights.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The moral cost of non-compliance is partly social.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation reflects both individual and collective moral stakes.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Laws coordinate behavior for mutual protection.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Coordination is a moral function.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Coordination requires reliability.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Reliability requires obligation rather than suggestion.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Thus law must be morally binding in structure.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Without moral bindingness, coordination collapses.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Without coordination, protections collapse.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Without protections, no legal obligation can exist.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Therefore legal obligation presupposes coordination-based moral goods.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal systems create stable expectations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Expectations themselves have moral significance.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Breaking rightful expectations wrongs others.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Compliance upholds the moral fabric of expectations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation thus includes an obligation to uphold rightful expectations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The system must be sufficiently good to justify expectations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Thresholds separate legitimate from illegitimate systems.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Moral thresholds determine the existence of legal obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Threshold-crossing is binary.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Below threshold: no legal obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Above threshold: legal obligation exists, regardless of degree.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Degree of goodness affects moral weight but not binary status.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A system can be barely legitimate or very legitimate; both impose obligations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obligations scale morally but not legally.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Moral obligation admits degrees; legal obligation does not.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Degrees of moral obligation determine how easily obligations are outweighed.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Outweighing explains moral conflicts with law.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Conflict does not undermine the identity of legal obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation remains a moral phenomenon embedded in a complex moral network.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation exists only where there is governmental protection.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal recognition requires protection.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal recognition is the conferral of protections.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Statements about laws possess normative content.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Statements about gunman-orders lack normative content.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Normative pregnancy distinguishes law from coercion.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The difference in normative content stems from moral background features.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Traffic laws reduce deaths and injuries.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Traffic laws do good even when individual infractions seem morally trivial.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The normativity of law is grounded in systemic benefit.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A gunman provides no systemic benefit.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A system that protects rights generates legal obligations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Typical legal systems protect most of a citizen’s rights.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal systems often prevent murder, enslavement, and dehumanization.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: This protective function explains why laws correspond to obligations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Even wicked laws presuppose a functioning government.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Government presupposes a fundamentally moral social structure.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Law’s normativity cannot be morally antiseptic.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: This remains true even in the presence of morally empty laws.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Some theories of legal obligation are plausible but false.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: One such false theory grounds legal obligation in moral obligations to those protected by government.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obeying evil laws often produces only harm.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Circuitous good effects do not justify obedience to oppressive laws.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: No categorical non-trivial good arises from obeying wicked laws.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Even benign laws show failure of the mistaken theory.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A legal obligation can exist even when fulfilling it benefits no one.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Driving 66 mph does not harm anyone the government protects.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation does not reduce to protecting beneficiaries.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Failing to drive 65 mph does not necessarily erode governmental authority.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Obligations to uphold government authority cannot explain legal obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation differs from obligation to avoid erosion of authority.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Recognition by a legal system is not mere acknowledgment of existence.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Recognition is a technical term involving standing.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Systems may refer to entities they do not legally recognize (e.g., pigs, Israel).
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal standing requires rights or protections.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Without protections, there is no legal standing.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Without standing, there is no legal obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal recognition is logically tied to protection.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A law that orders evil toward Smith does not constitute legal protection.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Such a system does not protect Smith and therefore does not recognize him.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal recognition requires conferral of protections even if the system mentions the person.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Stalinist denunciation laws produce legal obligations despite their wickedness.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: They do so because the Soviet state also provided many vital protections.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Citizens depended on Soviet law for protections against theft, rape, murder, etc.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Partial protection suffices for generating legal obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A gunman’s hostages receive no protections and thus no obligations to him.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A legal system can be created even by immoral agents.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Mutual fear among parties can generate a fair legal code.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Such a system is inherently moral in purpose.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal systems impose morality through their design.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Conceptual necessity may require laws to meet ethical standards.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Being consistent with morality is insufficient for full moral incorporation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Ethical principles are not constituents of legal systems as platonic entities.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Laws are temporal assurances, not abstract objects.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Assurances are spatiotemporal conditions, not events.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Issuing an assurance is an event; the assurance itself is a condition.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Laws come into and go out of existence.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Laws are non-platonic entities.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A law is an assurance of moral protection.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A legal system is a system of such assurances.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Laws have a basis in morality because assurances are moral acts.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: This avoids the absurdity of inserting moral principles into physical entities.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Nations can in principle have legal obligations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: There are currently no international laws.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: International law requires conditions not presently met.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A government is the main guarantor of rights in a given area.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligations arise only where the government provides vital protections.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation requires dependence on the government’s protections.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation exists only where moral protection exists.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Issuing a law is issuing an assurance of protection.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: International legal obligation would require an institution that guarantees rights of nations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Such an institution does not currently exist.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation is tied to jurisdictional boundaries.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Dependence determines jurisdictional legal duties.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A legal system must protect rights to generate legal duties.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Protection is necessary for the normative force of laws.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: The normative difference between commands and laws derives from moral purpose.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Systems with moral purpose create obligations; coercive individuals do not.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Wicked laws can still have normative force due to background protections.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation emerges from systemic features, not individual mandates.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal normativity tracks existence of a morally structured system.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Pathological laws rely on normal system functioning for their authority.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: A completely non-protective entity cannot issue laws.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Recognition confers legal personality.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Standing is necessary for obligation.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Without standing, one cannot be bound by laws.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation is tied to moral dependence on a system.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Systems protect differently situated individuals asymmetrically.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Even overridden obligations remain obligations.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Overriding does not imply nullification.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Principles have weight rather than binary force.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Principles can conflict without canceling one another.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligations behave like moral principles in weight.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Legal obligation can conflict with stronger moral duties.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation
source: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation (Kuczynski)

positions.text: Conflict explains appearance of law lacking moral force.
positions.title: The Moral Structure of Legal Obligation

positions.text: The concept of language is fundamentally tied to the concept of meaning, and understanding the nature of language requires distinguishing between different types of meaning.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Linguistic expressions are inherently meaningful, and the concept of meaning can be categorized into evidential, psychological, and linguistic meanings, with linguistic meaning being distinct and foundational.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: The core thesis of the text is that linguistic meaning and psychological meaning are distinct, with psychological meaning being parasitic on linguistic meaning.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: The text also argues that the relationship between a sentence and its meaning is conventional and not purely causal, distinguishing it from natural signs like smoke and fire.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: The text argues that sentences are structurally isomorphic to propositions, which are digital structures composed of discrete parts, and that the study of language can be divided into three branches: semantics, syntax, and pragmatics.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: The text posits that the meaning of a sentence is a proposition, which is distinct from the sentence itself but structurally similar, and that language use involves both literal and non-literal meanings, which are studied by semantics and pragmatics, respectively.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: The meaning of complex expressions in language is determined by the semantic structures of their constituent parts, and understanding these structures requires the discipline of semantics.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: The literal meaning of a sentence can be distinguished from what a speaker intends to convey, and semantics is essential for clarifying the structure of literal meaning, especially in cases where this distinction is not immediately clear.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: The literal meaning of a sentence is determined by the meanings of its simple parts and the way those parts are arranged. - This thesis is articulated throughout the text, particularly in sections discussing the relationship between simple and complex expressions.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: The literal meaning of simple expressions coincides with what people take them to mean, while the literal meaning of complex expressions may diverge from what people understand them to mean. - This formulation is evident in the text's discussion of how systematic error is impossible with simple expressions but possible with complex ones.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: The meaning of indexicals and definite descriptions is determined by rules that systematically assign referents based on the context of utterance.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Indexicals and definite descriptions have a two-dimensional semantic structure, where the meaning of a token is its referent, and the meaning of the type is the rule that assigns that referent.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: The meaning of a sentence token is a proposition, while the meaning of a sentence type is a rule that assigns propositions to its tokens based on the context of utterance.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Context-sensitive expressions are essential for expressing perspectival thoughts and beliefs, and an indexical-free language would be severely impoverished.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Semantic rules assign meanings to properties, and these meanings are themselves semantic rules that assign meanings to instances of those properties.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: All expressions, including those that appear context-insensitive, have a two-dimensional semantic structure where type-meaning diverges from token-meaning due to context-sensitivity.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Expression-types, unlike expression-tokens, are properties and thus non-spatiotemporal entities, which cannot mediate information or function as symbols. Therefore, semantic rules must assign meanings differently to types and tokens, necessitating a two-dimensional approach to semantics.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: The logical form of statements often diverges from their grammatical form, particularly in statements containing quantifiers, which are about properties rather than specific objects.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Quantified sentences in natural language are pseudo-objectual statements that appear to be about objects but are really about properties, necessitating a reparse to expose their logical forms. - This thesis is articulated in the opening sentence: "concerns properties. In natural language, quantified sentences are pseudo-objectual statements. They appear to be about objects, but are really about properties. That is why they must be reparsed if their logical forms are to be exposed."
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: The divergence between a statement's grammatical form and its logical form is a consequence of the need to define certain expressions contextually. - This is evident in the passage: "The divergence between SS's grammatical and logical forms is obviously a consequence of the fact that 'someone' must be defined contextually."
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Expressions are essential to language.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Expressions must be meaningful.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: The word 'meaning' has three distinct meanings: evidential, psychological, and linguistic.

positions.text: Linguistic meaning is distinct from and foundational to psychological meaning.

positions.text: Linguistic meaning is determined by convention.

positions.text: Psychological meaning depends on the speaker's understanding of linguistic meaning.

positions.text: The meanings of complex expressions are derived from the meanings of their simpler components.

positions.text: Understanding the structure of these meanings requires the discipline of semantics.

positions.text: Semantics is necessary to clarify the metaknowledge involved in understanding sentences.

positions.text: There is a distinction between what is literally meant by an utterance and what the speaker intends to convey.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Different perceptual descriptions can lead to the same literal meaning being grasped differently (illustrated by the Steve example).
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Degree properties like "smart" are contextually relative and can have different meanings in different contexts (illustrated by the Fido and Timmy example).
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Languages are sets of semantic rules that systematically pair sentences with meanings (general statement about language).
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: The literal meaning of simple expressions is determined by what people take them to mean.

positions.text: The literal meaning of complex expressions is a function of the meanings of their simple parts and the order in which those parts are arranged.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Systematic misinterpretations of sentences arise from people not knowing how to put the meanings of simple expressions together.

positions.text: Indexicals refer to different entities based on the context of utterance.

positions.text: Indexicals have a two-dimensional semantic structure, where the meaning of a token is its referent, and the meaning of the type is the rule that assigns that referent.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Different beliefs and thoughts about the same state of affairs can be held independently.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Context-sensitive expressions are necessary to express these independent beliefs and thoughts accurately.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Sentence types containing context-sensitive components do not bear truth values; instead, tokens of these types make statements and bear truth values based on the context of utterance.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: The semantic rule for context-sensitive expressions assigns propositions to tokens based on the context of utterance.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: All expressions in natural languages contain context-sensitive components, such as tense-markers.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: The meaning of an expression-type (type-meaning) is distinct from the meaning of its tokens (token-meaning) due to context-sensitivity.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Expression-types are properties and are non-spatiotemporal entities.

positions.text: Non-spatiotemporal entities cannot mediate information or function as symbols.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: There are profound metaphysical differences between tokens and types.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Some statements, given their grammatical forms, appear to be about objects but are in fact about properties.
positions.title: Analytic Philosophy Chapter 4

positions.text: Statements containing quantifiers are about properties rather than specific objects.

positions.text: The logical form of such statements diverges from their grammatical form.

positions.text: This divergence is due to the need to define certain expressions contextually.

positions.text: Frege discovered the divergence between grammatical and logical forms, which is foundational to modern analytic philosophy.

positions.text: Understanding language requires understanding the nature of linguistic meaning and its distinction from other types of meaning.

positions.text: The text concludes by reinforcing the centrality of linguistic meaning to the concept of language.

positions.text: Therefore, linguistic meaning and psychological meaning are distinct, with psychological meaning being parasitic on linguistic meaning.

positions.text: Therefore, semantics is essential for understanding the structure of meaning in language, particularly in distinguishing literal from non-literal meaning.
